title: Recruiter Screens, Written Communication, and Process Control · Opportunity Transfer Studio
summary: A different candidate, role family, location, access need, employment history, constraint, and hiring process requires an independent transfer of recruiter screens, written communication, and process control into a changed screening conversation and process record. Planned artifact: Produce an independent changed-context version of screening conversation and process record with automated and judgment evidence. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal…
objectives: Deliver a flexible evidence-grounded introduction connecting target work, relevant behavior, proof, and reason for this conversation.
Clarify ambiguous role, level, process, location, schedule, compensation, work sample, and decision criteria before answering.
Respond to irrelevant, sensitive, or jurisdiction-dependent questions by protecting privacy, redirecting to job function, and seeking qualified local guidance when needed.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# job-search cumulative learner-controlled career campaign
# Browser labs review fictional evidence contracts only. They never contact people,
# submit applications, access accounts, expose personal data, negotiate, or accept offers.
workspace: career
candidate-id: fictional-learner
target-role: [replace with role family, work outcomes, and bounded level]
jurisdiction: [replace with location and current official guidance boundary]
audience: [replace with reviewer need, time, format, and access context]
claim: [replace with one truthful role-relevant behavior claim]
evidence: [replace with revision, artifact, observation, changed case, contribution, and limit]
process-state: [replace with discover, investigate, apply, interview, offer, close, or archive]
accessibility: [replace with keyboard, structure, reflow, status, alternatives, and human review]
privacy-consent: [replace with purpose, minimization, permission, retention, deletion, and safe contact]
decision: [replace with learner-owned next decision and stop rule]
transfer-boundary: learner-approved external actions only
review:

steps.title: Commit to the mission model · Lab Concise Introduction
steps.instruction:
lab case — A rushed screen mixes role evidence, pay, location, authorization, access, sensitive history, unclear process, and an implied but unconfirmed next step. The lab variation 6 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Deliver a flexible evidence-grounded introduction connecting target work, relevant behavior, proof, and reason for this conversation.

What must be established before implementation?

Evidence target: Commit to the mission model · Lab Concise Introduction.

steps.title: Defend the transferred model · Lab Screen Close
steps.instruction:
Write the lab evidence note for Screen Close: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A rushed screen mixes role evidence, pay, location, authorization, access, sensitive history, unclear process, and an implied but unconfirmed next step. The lab variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.

Evidence target: Defend the transferred model · Lab Screen Close.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Defend the transferred model · Lab Screen Close, keep Screen Close connected to mechanically reviewable target-role, truthful-claim, contribution, accessibility, privacy, consent, process-state, changed-candidate, failure, repair, decision, and transfer evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.

hints: For Defend the transferred model · Lab Screen Close, name the learner goal, target role, jurisdiction, audience, truthful claim, contribution, source date, evidence revision, accessibility, privacy, consent, process state, decision owner, non-claim, and transfer boundary, Screen Close precondition, or invariant that must hold.

At Defend the transferred model · Lab Screen Close, trace this concrete input and predict targeting, source, evidence, contribution, access, privacy, consent, process, decision, rejection, repair, follow-up, and learner-controlled transfer evidence before reviewing it: A rushed screen mixes role evidence, pay, location, authorization, access, sensitive history, unclear process, and an implied but unconfirmed next step. The lab variation 5 changes the candidate background, target role, jurisdiction, access need, source date, process state, and decision constraint. Competency probe: Confirm mutual interest, unresolved questions, next step, owner, timing, artifact promises, contact channel, and follow-up record.

For Defend the transferred model · Lab Screen Close, challenge the documented misconception with changed-candidate, target-role, source-date, jurisdiction, audience, access, privacy, consent, process-state, interview, offer, rejection, repair, and learner-controlled transfer evidence: A friendly conversation implies advancement.
